# Intravitreal Injection Protocol

## Preparation

1. Verify patient identity and correct eye (surgical site marking)
2. Obtain informed consent
3. Instill topical anesthetic (proparacaine 0.5%)
4. Apply 5% povidone-iodine to conjunctival sac

## Procedure

1. Insert lid speculum
2. Measure 3.5-4.0 mm from limbus (pars plana entry)
3. Inject 0.05 mL of anti-VEGF agent using 30-gauge needle
4. Apply gentle pressure with cotton-tip at injection site

## Details

Post-injection, check for light perception and optic nerve perfusion. Intraocular pressure typically normalizes within 30 minutes. Instruct the patient to report any sudden vision loss, increasing pain, or floaters.

## Contraindications

- Active ocular or periocular infection
- Known hypersensitivity to the active substance
- Active intraocular inflammation (grade 3+ cells)
